A three-alarm fire in Brooklyn hospitalized five, while a surge in teen violence prompted Bronx DA calls for reforms; NYC's crime landscape remains tense with multiple shootings, and Mayor Mamdani seeks to end emergency shelter policies amid ongoing housing and health care challenges.

### Risks & Alerts

- Three-alarm Brooklyn apartment fire leaves five people hospitalized, according to FDNY. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTE42X1ZQTEJTTmNOMjZ2aDNQaERCU1F5S3NxdUFwSnIyajFaMHdDMnJOZEtQM09uVGV6Tk1Wb0Qtakx1T1Q3clZfd29uZ1VPTlNXQ0hzNVZ1aGVpZ3FJQVFwS20tQXlzYV9RcU94T1JTNDc4YUhycFk3ZEUxU2Y?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- Bronx DA calls for reforms after deadly shooting and surge in teen violence. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- NYC jury convicts suspect in Chinatown homeless murders, rejecting insanity defense. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Mamdani moves to end Rikers emergency orders and implement solitary reforms.
